mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and earnings revision risk, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate above 50 percent during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 3.5 percent, higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to achieve statistical significance in predicting 60-day returns, or the combined signal produces negative alpha in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling windows, or the signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below its base level of 100, it indicates a structural contraction in manufacturing capacity that depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases when INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold signal loses predictive power after adjusting for sector rotation effects.
